Defuddle on Cloudflare Workers #56

New issue
Copy link
New issue
Copy link
Open
Open
Defuddle on Cloudflare Workers#56
Copy link

Description

jmorrell
opened on May 25, 2025 · edited by jmorrell
Contributor
Issue body actions

Example repo here: https://github.com/jmorrell/defuddle-cloudflare-example

I was looking forward to ditching my tenuous readbilityjs fork in my workers project. Defuddle ultimately does run to completion, but I ran into a couple of issues. Supporting this environment fully is likely challenging since JSDOM does not work within the Worker environment. I suspect the same will be true of the deno and bun runtimes.

I was able to work around this by using the browser version along with linkedom, however this is not an exact replacement for JSDOM and doesn't implement all the CSS functionality.

Since defuddle relies on these style heuristics, I'm not sure there is a great path to supporting the full functionality in this environment, but I wanted to open an issue for discussion and to document these issues for anyone else who might hit this.

When running I get two errors:

Defuddle: Error evaluating media queries: TypeError: undefined is not iterable (cannot read property Symbol(Symbol.iterator))

This is due to linkedom not implementing doc.styleSheets:

defuddle/src/defuddle.ts

Line 213 in cb4291d

const sheets = Array.from(doc.styleSheets).filter(sheet => {

This could be silenced by falling back to [] if doc.styleSheets isn't present, however that may not be the desired behavior.

const sheets = Array.from(doc.styleSheets ?? [])

The second issue is due to getComputedStyle not being supported by linkedom.

Defuddle Error processing document: TypeError: e3.getComputedStyle is not a function

If you feel like there's nothing to do, or supporting Workers is out-of-scope for the project, feel free to close the issue

Activity

jmorrell commented on May 26, 2025

jmorrell
on May 26, 2025 · edited by jmorrell
ContributorAuthor

For my use-case, I'm already running a headless browser rendering worker to load the page anyway. Instead of downloading the HTML content from the browser and then trying to process it in the worker, I can load defuddle in the browser itself with the page loaded and execute it there. This is a little more awkward but seems to work pretty well!

For anyone who might be going down the same path I've created a minimal example here: https://github.com/jmorrell/defuddle-browser-worker-example

masylum commented on Jun 2, 2025

masylum
on Jun 2, 2025

it would be great to replace the dom calls to cheerio. I've done something similar porting readability to cheerio here: https://jsr.io/@paoramen/cheer-reader. @kepano would you be interested on merging this or should I just maintain my own fork if I do?

nbbaier commented on Jun 8, 2025

nbbaier
on Jun 8, 2025

Thanks for this @jmorrell. I'm also trying to use linkedom and was running into the same issues. Hope workers can be supported!

nbbaier commented on Jun 8, 2025

nbbaier
on Jun 8, 2025 · edited by nbbaier

I actually decided to see if I could use a coding agent to implement doc.styleSheets and getComputedStyle. I used opencode with Claude 4 Sonnet to do so and, at least in a small test in Bun (not on workers), it's working well. My fork of linkedom is here.

masylum commented on Jun 17, 2025

masylum
on Jun 17, 2025

wrong link to fork

nbbaier
on Jun 17, 2025

Oops! Yes, fixed in the original post. Thanks, @masylum
